#+title: Strata API Reference
#+author: Generated by tools/api-doc.lisp
#+startup: showall
#+options: toc:2

This file is generated automatically. Do not edit by hand.
Run the generator to refresh it:

#+begin_example
sbcl --non-interactive \
  --eval '(asdf:load-system :strata)' \
  --load tools/api-doc.lisp \
  --eval '(strata/tools/api-doc:generate)'
#+end_example

Entries marked /Undocumented/ are exported symbols that still
need a docstring added.

* Package =strata.app=

** Functions

*** =connect-db=

#+begin_example
(connect-db &key (database "strata_dev") (user "strata") (password "localtest123") (host "localhost") (port 5432))
#+end_example

Connect the PostgreSQL backend and ensure all tables exist.

*** =make-app=

#+begin_example
(make-app &key (port 4242))
#+end_example

Create the Fluxion application instance and register per-session component factories.
The shell-component factory is registered here so that every new browser
session automatically gets its own shell-component instance. Fluxion's
dispatch path looks up the component from the session when routing actions
to /action/strata-shell/<action-name>.

** Variables

*** =*app*=

The active Fluxion application instance.

*** =*db-backend*=

The active Fluxion PostgreSQL backend instance.

* Package =strata.auth=

** Functions

*** =any-users-p=

#+begin_example
(any-users-p)
#+end_example

Return T if at least one user exists in the database, NIL otherwise.
Used by the page handler guard to decide whether to redirect to /setup.

*** =setup-user-tables=

#+begin_example
(setup-user-tables)
#+end_example

Ensure fluxion.user tables exist. Idempotent; call during app startup.

*** =update-password=

#+begin_example
(update-password username new-password)
#+end_example

Update the password hash for USERNAME to a hash of NEW-PASSWORD.
Verification of the current password must be done by the caller before
invoking this function.

*** =user-display-name=

#+begin_example
(user-display-name user-alist)
#+end_example

Return a human-readable name for USER-ALIST.
Tries the display_name extensible field first, then falls back to username.

*** =user-id-from-session=

#+begin_example
(user-id-from-session session)
#+end_example

Return the integer user ID from SESSION, or 0 if not authenticated.

* Package =strata.components.shell=

** Classes

*** =shell-component=

Top-level Fluxion component that renders the full Strata application shell.
Manages the active channel selection and the open thread pane state.
The rendered HTML is a three-column grid: workspace rail, channel sidebar,
and main panel (channel header, post feed, composer).
Actions handled: switch-channel, post, open-thread.

** Functions

*** =make-shell=

#+begin_example
(make-shell)
#+end_example

Create a fresh shell-component instance for use as a per-session factory.
Called by the factory registered in strata.app:make-app via
fluxion.server:register-component-factory.

*** =render-page-for-session=

#+begin_example
(render-page-for-session session)
#+end_example

Render the full Strata HTML page for SESSION.
Retrieve the shell component from the session registry (put there by the
factory registered at startup) and render the full page via Fluxion.

* Package =strata.components.thread=

** Functions

*** =render-thread-pane=

#+begin_example
(render-thread-pane post-id shell-component-id session)
#+end_example

Return an HTML string for the thread pane showing POST-ID's replies.
SHELL-COMPONENT-ID is the DOM id of the enclosing shell component, used
to build action URLs. SESSION is the current Fluxion session.

* Package =strata.jobs.notifications=

** Functions

*** =start-notification-hooks=

#+begin_example
(start-notification-hooks)
#+end_example

Register the :mention and :post-touched hooks.  Call once at startup.

* Package =strata.migrations=

** Functions

*** =ensure-schema=

#+begin_example
(ensure-schema)
#+end_example

Create all Strata tables if they do not already exist.
Safe to call on every startup. Adding a new table here and restarting
is all that is required to extend the schema during development.

* Package =strata.models.bookmark=

** Functions

*** =add-bookmark=

#+begin_example
(add-bookmark &key user-id post-id note due-date)
#+end_example

Save a bookmark of POST-ID for USER-ID and return the data-model.
USER-ID and POST-ID are integer _ids.
NOTE is an optional freetext annotation; defaults to an empty string.
DUE-DATE is an optional ISO-8601 date string used for reminder sorting.
Does not enforce uniqueness; callers should call remove-bookmark first
if replacing an existing bookmark on the same post.

*** =bookmark-field=

#+begin_example
(bookmark-field bookmark field)
#+end_example

Return the value of FIELD in the BOOKMARK data-model object.
FIELD is a column name string, e.g. "note", "due_date", "post_id".

*** =bookmark-p=

#+begin_example
(bookmark-p user-id post-id)
#+end_example

Return T if USER-ID has bookmarked POST-ID, NIL otherwise.

*** =list-bookmarks-for-user=

#+begin_example
(list-bookmarks-for-user user-id &key (limit 50))
#+end_example

Return up to LIMIT bookmarks for USER-ID, ordered by due_date ASC then created_at DESC.
Bookmarks with a due_date sort to the top so time-sensitive reminders appear first.
LIMIT defaults to 50.

*** =remove-bookmark=

#+begin_example
(remove-bookmark &key user-id post-id)
#+end_example

Delete the bookmark record for USER-ID on POST-ID.
Is a no-op if no such record exists.

* Package =strata.models.channel=

** Functions

*** =channel-field=

#+begin_example
(channel-field channel field)
#+end_example

Return the value of FIELD in the CHANNEL data-model object.
FIELD is a column name string (e.g. "name", "kind", "last_activity").

*** =create-channel=

#+begin_example
(create-channel &key workspace-id slug name description (kind "open") parent-channel-id)
#+end_example

Create and persist a new channel record. Returns the saved data-model.
WORKSPACE-ID is the _id of the owning workspace.
SLUG is a URL-safe identifier unique within the workspace.
NAME is the display name shown in the channel list.
DESCRIPTION is optional freetext; defaults to an empty string.
KIND is "open" (default), "private", or "dm".
Initial visibility is "private"; last_activity is set to now.

*** =find-channel-by-id=

#+begin_example
(find-channel-by-id id)
#+end_example

Return the channel data-model whose _id equals the integer ID, or NIL.

*** =find-channel-by-slug=

#+begin_example
(find-channel-by-slug workspace-id slug)
#+end_example

Return the channel data-model with SLUG in workspace WORKSPACE-ID, or NIL.
Slugs are unique per workspace, not globally.

*** =list-channels-for-user=

#+begin_example
(list-channels-for-user workspace-id user-id member-channel-ids)
#+end_example

Return channels visible to USER-ID in WORKSPACE-ID.
Open channels are always included. Private channels are included only
if their _id appears in MEMBER-CHANNEL-IDS (a list of integers).
Only top-level channels are returned; use list-subchannels for children.

*** =list-channels-for-workspace=

#+begin_example
(list-channels-for-workspace workspace-id)
#+end_example

Return top-level channels in WORKSPACE-ID ordered by last_activity descending.
Only returns channels with no parent (parent_channel_id IS NULL), so sub-channels
are not shown at the top level. Use list-subchannels to get children.

*** =list-subchannels=

#+begin_example
(list-subchannels parent-channel-id)
#+end_example

Return all direct child channels of PARENT-CHANNEL-ID, ordered by name.

*** =touch-channel=

#+begin_example
(touch-channel channel-id)
#+end_example

Set last_activity on CHANNEL-ID to the current universal time and persist it.
Call this whenever a post is created or replied to in the channel so the
channel sidebar ordering stays fresh.

* Package =strata.models.channel-member=

** Functions

*** =add-member=

#+begin_example
(add-member channel-id user-id &key (role "member"))
#+end_example

Add USER-ID to CHANNEL-ID with ROLE (default "member").
Role may be "member" or "admin". Duplicate entries are not checked here;
callers should verify membership first with member-p.

*** =list-channels-for-user=

#+begin_example
(list-channels-for-user user-id)
#+end_example

Return all channel_id integers that USER-ID is explicitly a member of.

*** =list-members=

#+begin_example
(list-members channel-id)
#+end_example

Return all membership records for CHANNEL-ID, ordered by joined_at ascending.

*** =member-field=

#+begin_example
(member-field member-record field)
#+end_example

Return the value of FIELD from a CHANNEL-MEMBERS data-model object.

*** =member-p=

#+begin_example
(member-p channel-id user-id)
#+end_example

Return T if USER-ID is a member of CHANNEL-ID, NIL otherwise.

*** =remove-member=

#+begin_example
(remove-member channel-id user-id)
#+end_example

Remove USER-ID from CHANNEL-ID. No-op if not a member.

* Package =strata.models.channel-read=

** Functions

*** =channel-read-field=

#+begin_example
(channel-read-field record field)
#+end_example

Return the value of FIELD in the channel-read RECORD data-model.
FIELD is a column name string, e.g. "last_read_post_id", "updated_at".

*** =get-channel-read=

#+begin_example
(get-channel-read user-id channel-id)
#+end_example

Return the channel-read data-model for USER-ID in CHANNEL-ID, or NIL.
Returns NIL if the user has never read the channel (treat as all-unread).

*** =mark-channel-read=

#+begin_example
(mark-channel-read &key user-id channel-id last-post-id)
#+end_example

Upsert the read receipt for USER-ID in CHANNEL-ID.
LAST-POST-ID is the _id of the most recent post the user has seen.
If a record already exists for the (user-id, channel-id) pair it is updated
in-place; otherwise a new record is inserted. updated_at is always set to now.
This drives the unread count badge in the channel sidebar.

* Package =strata.models.mention=

** Functions

*** =list-mentions-for-user=

#+begin_example
(list-mentions-for-user user-id &key (limit 50))
#+end_example

Return up to LIMIT recent mentions for USER-ID, ordered newest first.
Used to populate the @mentions inbox view. LIMIT defaults to 50.

*** =mention-field=

#+begin_example
(mention-field mention field)
#+end_example

Return the value of FIELD in the MENTION data-model object.
FIELD is a column name string, e.g. "mentioned_user_id", "post_id".

*** =parse-and-record-mentions=

#+begin_example
(parse-and-record-mentions &key post-id reply-id body channel-name poster-name)
#+end_example

Extract @username tokens from BODY, record each mention, and fire the
:mention hook so push notifications are sent.  CHANNEL-NAME and POSTER-NAME
are used as display strings in the push notification payload.
Safe to call when DB or hooks are unavailable; errors are silently ignored.

*** =record-mention=

#+begin_example
(record-mention &key post-id reply-id mentioned-user-id)
#+end_example

Record a parsed @mention and return the saved data-model.
Exactly one of POST-ID or REPLY-ID should be non-nil; the other is stored as 0.
MENTIONED-USER-ID is the _id of the user who was mentioned.
Call this from the post/reply creation path after parsing @name tokens
from the body text to drive notification queries.

* Package =strata.models.post=

** Functions

*** =create-post=

#+begin_example
(create-post &key channel-id author-id kind body (status "open") pinned)
#+end_example

Create and persist a new post record. Returns the saved data-model.
CHANNEL-ID is the _id of the channel this post belongs to.
AUTHOR-ID is the _id of the posting user.
KIND must be one of +post-kinds+: "message" (default), "question",
  "decision", "announcement", "task", "poll", or "note".
BODY is the plain-text (or lightly marked-up) content.
STATUS defaults to "open"; valid values are in +post-statuses+.
PINNED, if non-nil, pins the post to the top of the feed.
Fires :post-created with the new post's _id if that hook is registered.

*** =find-post-by-id=

#+begin_example
(find-post-by-id id)
#+end_example

Return the post data-model whose _id equals the integer ID, or NIL.

*** =list-posts-for-channel=

#+begin_example
(list-posts-for-channel channel-id &key include-resolved (limit 50))
#+end_example

Return posts for CHANNEL-ID sorted by pinned DESC then last_activity DESC.
By default posts with status "resolved", "decided", or "done" are excluded;
pass INCLUDE-RESOLVED t to include them.
LIMIT caps the result set; defaults to 50. This is the primary feed query.

*** =post-field=

#+begin_example
(post-field post field)
#+end_example

Return the value of FIELD in the POST data-model object.
FIELD is a column name string, e.g. "body", "kind", "status", "last_activity".

*** =set-post-status=

#+begin_example
(set-post-status post-id status)
#+end_example

Set the status field of POST-ID to STATUS and call touch-post.
STATUS must be one of +post-statuses+: "open", "resolved", "decided", "done".
Setting a non-open status will cause the post to disappear from the default
feed view on the next render (list-posts-for-channel excludes them).

*** =touch-post=

#+begin_example
(touch-post post-id)
#+end_example

Update last_activity on POST-ID to now and persist it, then fire :post-touched.
This is the single write path all mutations go through to resurface a post in
the feed. Replies, reactions, and status changes all call this so the channel
feed ordering always reflects the most recent activity on a thread.
If the :post-touched hook has not been registered this call is a no-op for
the hook but the database update still happens.

** Variables

*** =+post-kinds+=

Valid post kind values.

*** =+post-statuses+=

Valid post status values.

* Package =strata.models.push-subscription=

** Functions

*** =delete-subscription=

#+begin_example
(delete-subscription endpoint)
#+end_example

Remove the push subscription with ENDPOINT.

*** =find-by-endpoint=

#+begin_example
(find-by-endpoint endpoint)
#+end_example

Return the subscription record matching ENDPOINT, or NIL.

*** =list-for-user=

#+begin_example
(list-for-user user-id)
#+end_example

Return all push subscription records for USER-ID.

*** =save-subscription=

#+begin_example
(save-subscription user-id endpoint p256dh auth-key)
#+end_example

Persist a Web Push subscription for USER-ID.
ENDPOINT is the push service URL from the browser PushSubscription object.
P256DH is the base64url-encoded client public key.
AUTH-KEY is the base64url-encoded auth secret.
Replaces any existing subscription with the same endpoint.

*** =subscription-field=

#+begin_example
(subscription-field sub field)
#+end_example

Return the value of FIELD from a PUSH-SUBSCRIPTIONS data-model object.

* Package =strata.models.reaction=

** Functions

*** =add-reaction=

#+begin_example
(add-reaction &key target-type target-id user-id emoji)
#+end_example

Add an emoji reaction and return the saved data-model.
TARGET-TYPE must be "post" or "reply".
TARGET-ID is the _id of the target object.
USER-ID is the _id of the reacting user.
EMOJI is the emoji character string, e.g. "👍".
If TARGET-TYPE is "post", touch-post is called so the post resurfaces.
Does not deduplicate; callers should check for an existing reaction first.

*** =list-reactions-for-target=

#+begin_example
(list-reactions-for-target target-type target-id)
#+end_example

Return all reaction data-models for TARGET-ID of TARGET-TYPE.
Ordered by emoji then created_at so grouped emoji counts are easy to compute.

*** =reaction-field=

#+begin_example
(reaction-field reaction field)
#+end_example

Return the value of FIELD in the REACTION data-model object.
FIELD is a column name string, e.g. "emoji", "user_id", "target_type".

*** =remove-reaction=

#+begin_example
(remove-reaction &key target-type target-id user-id emoji)
#+end_example

Delete the matching reaction record. Calls touch-post if TARGET-TYPE is "post".
All four keyword arguments must match the stored record exactly.

* Package =strata.models.reply=

** Functions

*** =create-reply=

#+begin_example
(create-reply &key post-id author-id body)
#+end_example

Create and persist a reply to POST-ID. Returns the saved data-model.
POST-ID is the _id of the post being replied to.
AUTHOR-ID is the _id of the replying user.
BODY is the plain-text reply content.
Always calls touch-post on POST-ID so the parent resurfaces in the feed.

*** =find-reply-by-id=

#+begin_example
(find-reply-by-id id)
#+end_example

Return the reply data-model whose _id equals the integer ID, or NIL.

*** =list-replies-for-post=

#+begin_example
(list-replies-for-post post-id)
#+end_example

Return all replies for POST-ID sorted by created_at ascending.
This is the ordering used in the thread pane: oldest reply first.

*** =reply-field=

#+begin_example
(reply-field reply field)
#+end_example

Return the value of FIELD in the REPLY data-model object.
FIELD is a column name string, e.g. "body", "author_id", "post_id".

* Package =strata.models.workspace=

** Functions

*** =create-workspace=

#+begin_example
(create-workspace &key slug display-name)
#+end_example

Create and persist a new workspace record. Returns the saved data-model.
SLUG is a URL-safe identifier (e.g. "acme-corp").
DISPLAY-NAME is the human-readable title shown in the workspace rail.
Initial visibility is set to "private"; change it via a direct field update
if public workspaces are needed. The created_at field is set to the current
universal time.

*** =find-workspace-by-id=

#+begin_example
(find-workspace-by-id id)
#+end_example

Return the workspace data-model whose _id equals the integer ID, or NIL.
ID is the auto-incremented primary key assigned by the database on insert.

*** =find-workspace-by-slug=

#+begin_example
(find-workspace-by-slug slug)
#+end_example

Return the workspace data-model with the given SLUG string, or NIL.
Slugs are unique per installation and are used in URLs and config.

*** =list-workspaces=

#+begin_example
(list-workspaces)
#+end_example

Return all workspace data-models, sorted alphabetically by display_name.
Used to populate the workspace rail in the shell UI.

*** =workspace-field=

#+begin_example
(workspace-field workspace field)
#+end_example

Return the value of FIELD in the WORKSPACE data-model object.
FIELD is a column name string (e.g. "slug", "display_name").
This is a thin accessor wrapper over fxdm:model-field.

* Package =strata.push=

** Functions

*** =ensure-vapid-keypair=

#+begin_example
(ensure-vapid-keypair)
#+end_example

Load the VAPID keypair from disk; generate and save a fresh one if not present.

*** =notify-mentioned-users=

#+begin_example
(notify-mentioned-users mention-user-ids channel-name poster-name)
#+end_example

Send push notifications to all users in MENTION-USER-IDS.
Called from the post/reply hook when mentions are detected.

*** =notify-user=

#+begin_example
(notify-user user-id title body)
#+end_example

Send a push notification to all subscriptions for USER-ID.

*** =send-push=

#+begin_example
(send-push subscription-record title body &key (icon "/static/icons/icon-192.png"))
#+end_example

Send a Web Push notification to SUBSCRIPTION-RECORD.
SUBSCRIPTION-RECORD is a push_subscriptions data-model object.
TITLE and BODY are the notification text strings.
Returns T on success, NIL (with a logged error) on failure.
Note: this implementation sends an unencrypted payload for simplicity.
RFC 8291 content encryption (aes128gcm) should be added for production.

** Variables

*** =*vapid-public-key-b64*=

Base64url encoding of *vapid-public-key-bytes*. Passed to the JS
  pushManager.subscribe() call and sent in the Authorization header.

* Package =strata.server=

** Functions

*** =main=

#+begin_example
(main)
#+end_example

Binary entry point. Parses command-line arguments and calls RUN.
Recognised flags (all optional, defaults from *port* and strata.app:connect-db):
  --port PORT           HTTP listen port (default 4242)
  --db-name NAME        PostgreSQL database name (default strata_dev)
  --db-user USER        PostgreSQL user (default strata)
  --db-password PASS    PostgreSQL password
  --db-host HOST        PostgreSQL host (default localhost)
  --db-port PORT        PostgreSQL port (default 5432)

*** =run=

#+begin_example
(run &rest args &key (port *port*) &allow-other-keys)
#+end_example

Start Strata and block the calling thread until interrupted.
 Use this to run Strata from a terminal or script.

*** =start=

#+begin_example
(start &key (port *port*) (db-name "strata_dev") (db-user "strata") (db-password "localtest123") (db-host "localhost") (db-port 5432))
#+end_example

Start Strata: connect to PostgreSQL, run migrations, start the Woo HTTP server.

*** =stop=

#+begin_example
(stop)
#+end_example

Stop the Strata server and disconnect from the database.

** Variables

*** =*port*=

Default HTTP port for the Strata server.
